calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-20-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enter Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-27-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion to enter default and default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 09:43:59 a.m. and concluding at 09:55:32 a.m.

Plaintiff JPMorgan Chase Bank represented by Adam Brumage, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Rujira Yost not appearing.

MARIANA ECHEVERRIA-LICONA (Associate Vendor Management Analyst & Custodian of Records) is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents summary of case and an offer of proof.

The following exhibits are MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 1 - Application
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 2 - Account Statements

MARIANA ECHEVERRIA-LICONA is examined by the Court.

The matter is submitted.
By the statements made by Plaintiff's witness and its counsel, documents filed, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence proving each element of the complaint.

Court awards damages in the amount of $34,349.86 based upon the last payment made by Defendant on Aug-28-2021.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff JPMorgan Chase Bank against Defendant Rujira Yost, as follows:

1. Damages: $34,349.86

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $34,349.86

Proposed judgment signed and filed as modified.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-26-2023 Continued To Apr-12-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
